title: Diaphragmatic (Belly) Breathing
blurb: Full, low breathing that switches on calm.
lead: Diaphragmatic breathing — breathing deep into the belly rather than the chest — is a well-established foundation for relaxation and stress relief.

blocks.text: Diaphragmatic breathing means breathing fully using the diaphragm, so your belly expands rather than your chest rising and shoulders lifting.
It's the basis of most relaxation breathing and is well supported for reducing the physical stress response. Many people breathe shallowly when stressed; this retrains a calmer pattern.

items: Sit or lie down. Place one hand on your chest, one on your belly.
Breathe in slowly through your nose, directing the breath low so your belly hand rises more than your chest hand.
Exhale slowly, feeling the belly fall.
Keep the chest relatively still and the breath smooth.
Practise a few minutes daily so it becomes your default under stress.

items.label: Best for
items.value: Stress, anxiety, foundation for other techniques

blocks.text: Heaving the chest and shoulders. The movement should be low and gentle in the belly.

resources.title: Breathing exercises for stress
resources.whyHere: Free, trustworthy instructions for calming belly breathing.
